behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires proving an externally visible side effect (invalid row is actually present in the DLQ) and preserving row-number metadata across module boundaries (validator -> mapper -> DLQ writer). Unit-only tests can validate validation logic, but can miss real queue write/readback and payload-shape issues. Rejected higher level: Agentic is disqualified because no AI/ML output is involved; Workflow is disqualified because there are no multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline against a real DLQ backend with a CSV containing known missing-field rows, then read back DLQ messages and assert each rejected row includes the original CSV row number and original payload.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is pure transformation/parsing logic at a function/object boundary with no required I/O or external contract. Additional higher-level tests add little new reality signal beyond existing unit vectors for format coverage. Rejected higher levels: Integration/System are disqualified by behavior text focusing only on conversion correctness, not module handoff or infrastructure; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model call exists.
behaviors.test_description: Use table-driven unit tests for representative ISO 8601/US/EU inputs (including edge cases) and assert exact canonical output strings.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on runtime/OS characteristics (memory pressure, timeout under large load), which mocks cannot faithfully reproduce. Unit-only (or narrow integration) can miss allocation patterns, GC behavior, and end-to-end throughput constraints. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML decision is part of correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full pipeline process with a 500k-row fixture under production-like timeout/memory settings, and assert successful completion before timeout plus no OOM/crash while verifying final processed counts.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly targets a real external error-path contract (transient 503) and retry behavior under that contract; mocks often hide SDK/network error-shape nuances. A system test gives the needed reality check that the batch survives and backoff/retry policy works against actual S3-compatible responses. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model output quality or multi-agent sequence is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Against real S3 or a protocol-faithful S3 endpoint, inject a transient 503 on initial reads, observe retry attempts with increasing delays, then confirm batch completes without global failure.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: This requires proving an externally visible persistence side effect via round-trip DB readback across a real restart boundary. Unit tests cannot expose restart/idempotency failures caused by transaction boundaries, commit timing, or process interruption semantics. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness does not depend on AI/ML outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against real PostgreSQL, force a mid-batch process restart, resume processing, then query the target table and assert no duplicate logical rows (e.g., by business key) and correct total row count.
